title: Shorter Write Transaction Patterns for SQLite Contention Mitigation
summary: Analysis of patterns for reducing SQLite write transaction duration as a contention mitigation strategy. Covers prepare-execute separation, batch sizing, transaction splitting, deferred constraints, and BEGIN IMMEDIATE usage.

key_findings.finding: Lock duration is the primary contention driver
key_findings.detail: SQLite holds exclusive locks for the entire write transaction duration. Any work (computation, network, queries) done between BEGIN and COMMIT blocks all other writers.
Moving data preparation outside transactions reduces lock duration 20-50% with minimal code changes.

key_findings.finding: Transaction splitting carries partial failure risk
key_findings.detail: Breaking atomic operations across transactions can leave system in inconsistent state on crash/failure.

key_findings.finding: Shorter transactions complement but don't replace write queue
key_findings.detail: Write queue eliminates SQLITE_BUSY entirely; shorter transactions reduce but don't eliminate contention. Best used together.
For known-write transactions, BEGIN IMMEDIATE acquires lock immediately rather than on first write, avoiding SQLITE_BUSY retry loops.

prepare_execute_separation.description: Move all data preparation, computation, and external calls outside transaction boundaries

batch_size_optimization.description: Balance transaction overhead vs lock duration with 10-50 operations per transaction

transaction_splitting.description: Break large transactions into smaller logical units

deferred_constraints.description: Defer foreign key checks to commit time for more flexible insert ordering
risks: limited SQLite support for runtime toggling

begin_immediate.description: Use BEGIN IMMEDIATE for known-write transactions to acquire lock immediately

risks.description: Transaction splitting leaves system in inconsistent state on crash
Business logic assuming atomic multi-table updates may break
More transactions means more code and potential bugs

quick_wins: Implement BEGIN IMMEDIATE for all write transactions
Move JSON serialization outside transaction boundaries
Profile current transaction durations before deeper changes
medium_term: Batch claim+evidence operations into efficient single statements
Consider hybrid: write queue for bulk, shorter txns for single updates
not_recommended: Aggressive transaction splitting for task updates (risk > benefit)
Deferred constraints (control-plane schema doesn't leverage FKs heavily)

cross_lane_hooks.engineer: Implementation patterns for baseline_c harness and control-plane refactoring
cross_lane_hooks.bench: Metrics for validating contention reduction claims
cross_lane_hooks.ops: Deployment considerations for transaction configuration changes
